down the slide. Wheeee!

Benny was hungry, so he looked for something to eat. He found a big, red radish. It was so yummy! Benny ate the whole radish.

After eating, Benny felt sleepy. He went to his regular spot under a bush and took a nap. When he woke up, it was time for dinner. Benny went back to the garden to look for more radishes. The end.

prefix: One day, a child found a wrap. She was so excited about it. She had heard that popular people had wraps like these. She wanted to gain the wrap's power, so she
gold_continuation:
put it on.

When she put on the wrap, the child suddenly felt very popular. Everywhere she went, grown-ups smiled at her and funny things happened to her. She felt so happy!

The wrap stayed with the child forever. Whenever she wanted to gain a new power, all she had to do was put on the wrap. Children everywhere wanted the wrap, because it made them feel popular, special and loved.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy was very stubborn and always wanted his way. One day, Timmy's mom took him to the park to play on the swings. Timmy
gold_continuation:
wanted to swing very high, but his mom told him to be careful and not go too high.

Timmy didn't listen to his mom and kept swinging higher and higher. Suddenly, he fell off the swing and hit his head on the ground. His mom rushed him to the hospital, but the doctors couldn't save him.

Timmy's mom was very sad and wished she had recorded their last conversation at the park. She also wished she had made him sit in a safer seat on the swing. From that day on, Timmy's mom always made sure to record their conversations and made sure Timmy sat safely on all his swings.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a cute little kitten named Mittens. Mittens had a soft, fluffy paw that she loved to show off to her friends. One day, Mittens was playing with her friend
gold_continuation:
, a little mouse named Squeaky. They were having so much fun chasing each other around the garden.

Suddenly, Mittens stopped and looked at Squeaky with a serious face. "Do you hear that?" she asked. Squeaky listened carefully and heard a loud noise coming from the bushes. "What could it be?" Squeaky asked.

Mittens slowly walked towards the bushes and then suddenly jumped back. "It's a big, scary dog!" she cried. "We need to lay low and be very quiet until it goes away." Squeaky nodded and they both hid behind a nearby flower pot.

After a few minutes, the dog walked away and Mittens and Squeaky were safe. "Phew, that was not a normal day," Mittens said, as they continued to play together. From that day on, they always made sure to be careful and keep an eye out for any danger.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a favorite cushion that she loved to hug. One day, Lily was playing with her toys when she accidentally fell off her
gold_continuation:
chair. She was scared and started to cry, but her cushion was there to catch her. She felt safe and hugged her cushion tight. 

Lily's mom saw what happened and came to check on her. She asked if she was okay and Lily nodded her head. Her mom was happy to see that Lily was safe and sound. From that day on, Lily made sure to always have her cushion nearby when she was playing.

prefix: Once upon a time there was a mommy and a little girl. The mommy wanted to introduce her daughter to a new food. The food was called cereal. It was a very delicate cereal
gold_continuation: . The girl was very excited to try it. She tasted it and was pleased! She ate all the cereal and asked for more! She loved the cereal so much that she asked for it every day for breakfast. The end.

prefix: Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play in their room. They had many toys and clothes, but they did not like to tidy up. They threw their toys in a big sack and hid it
gold_continuation:
under the bed. They hung their clothes on a hanger and put it in the closet. They thought their mom would not see the mess.

One day, mom said they had to go to grandma's house for a visit. She told them to pack their things in a small bag. Lily and Ben ran to their room and opened the closet. They saw the hanger with their clothes, but it was empty. The clothes had fallen off the hanger and were wrinkled and dirty. They opened the sack under the bed, but it was full of toys. They could not find any clean clothes to wear.

They went to mom and said they had nothing to wear. Mom was angry and said they had to learn to take care of their things. She said they had to wear their old red pajamas to grandma's house. Lily and Ben felt ashamed and sad. They wished they had tidied up their room.

At grandma's house, Lily and Ben saw their cousins, who were wearing nice clothes and had neat bags. Their cousins laughed at them and said they looked silly in their red pajamas. Lily and Ben felt even more ashamed and sad. They wished they had listened to mom.

Grandma saw them and hugged them. She said she loved them no matter what they wore. She said she had a surprise for them. She took them to a room where she had made them new clothes from her old fabrics. She said she had used the hanger and the sack that they had left at her house last time. She said she had turned them into something beautiful and useful. She said she hoped they would like them and take care of them.

Lily and Ben were amazed and happy. They thanked grandma and tried on their new clothes. They fit them perfectly and had bright colors and patterns. They felt proud and happy. They learned that they should not waste their things and that they should tidy up their room. They also learned that grandma was very clever and kind. They hugged grandma and said they loved her. They decided to give some of their toys and clothes to their cousins, who were sorry for laughing at them. They all played together and had fun.

prefix: Tim and Sam were twins who liked to play outside. One day, they saw a big cane in the snow. It was shiny and red and had a hook at the end. They wanted to
gold_continuation:
touch it and see what it was.

They ran to the cane and grabbed it. It was very cold and icy. They felt a sharp pain in their hands. They looked and saw little teeth marks. The cane was not a cane, but a snake! It was hiding in the snow and bit them when they touched it.

They screamed and dropped the snake. They ran back to their house and told their mom what happened. She was very scared and angry. She wrapped their hands in a cloth and took them to the doctor. The doctor gave them some medicine and said they would be okay.

Tim and Sam learned a lesson. They should not touch things they do not know. They should be careful when they play outside. They should listen to their mom and stay away from snakes.

prefix: Once upon a time there was a stupid little inch. He was so small he could hardly be seen! But he wanted to prove to the world he was capable of doing amazing things
gold_continuation:
.

So, one day he decided to vanish into thin air. He waved goodbye and said "see you soon!". And with a poof he was gone!

The inch flew around the world, meeting all sorts of exciting people and creatures. He even saw a dragon. Everyone was very impressed when they discovered the little inch had vanished right before their eyes!

The inch had the time of his life. But finally it was time to go home. With a snap of his fingers, he vanished once again. He reappeared in his own backyard. 

The inch was so proud. He had proven to the world that even being so small, he could do big things! He had proved he was capable of much more than people thought.

prefix:
Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big crane. The crane was very sleepy. It had a long neck and could reach very high.

One day, the people
gold_continuation:
in the town wanted to decorate a tall tree. They put pretty things on the tree, like shiny balls and bright lights. But they could not reach the top of the tree.

They asked the sleepy crane for help. The crane was happy to help. It stretched its long neck up, up, up to the top of the tree. The people put the pretty things on the crane, and the crane put them on the tree.

The tree looked very pretty when it was all done. The people were so happy, and they said "thank you" to the sleepy crane. The crane felt good for helping and could now go to sleep. Everyone in the town had a great time, and they all lived happily ever after.

prefix:
Dennis and his mom were walking down the street when they spotted a cat. It was a fast cat. Dennis was so excited. 

He asked his mom, "
gold_continuation:
Can I lay with it? Please, Mama, can I lay with it?"

His mom said, "No, Dennis. We can't be too close." 

But Dennis didn't listen and he moved closer to the cat. He slowly reached out so he could lay with it. But before he could do it, the cat ran away. Dennis was so sad because he wanted to lay with it so badly. 

He started to cry, and said to his mom, "It was too fast, Mama! I wanted to lay with it." 

His mom hugged him and said, "I'm sorry, Dennis. Maybe next time." 

But there won't be a next time. The cat was gone, and Dennis will never lay with it.

prefix: Sara and Ben liked to play in the park. They liked to slide, swing, and run. One day, they saw a big pile of ash near a bench. It was dry and black.
gold_continuation:
"What is this?" Sara asked.

"I don't know. Maybe it is dirt," Ben said.

"Let's play with it. It looks fun," Sara said.

She took a stick and poked the ash. It made a cloud of dust. She coughed and rubbed her eyes.

"Ow, it hurts!" she cried.

Ben tried to help her. He gave her his water bottle. She drank some and washed her face.

"Are you okay?" he asked.

"I think so. But the ash is not fun. It is bad," she said.

They heard a voice behind them. It was a man in a uniform. He had a hose and a helmet.

"Hey, kids, what are you doing here? This is not a place to play. This is the remains of a fire. It is dangerous and dirty. You could get hurt or sick," he said.

He sprayed some water on the ash. It made a hissing sound.

"Sorry, sir. We didn't know. We were curious," Ben said.

"Curiosity is good, but you have to be careful. Always ask an adult before you touch something you don't know. And stay away from fire and ash. They can burn and harm you," he said.

He led them to their parents, who were waiting for them. They hugged them and checked them.

"We're sorry, mom and dad. We learned our lesson. We won't play with ash again," Sara and Ben said.

"That's good, kids. We're glad you're safe. And we're proud of you for telling the truth and apologizing. Now let's go home and have some ice cream," their parents said.

They smiled and followed them. They were happy and grateful. They learned to be careful and respectful. And they still liked to play in the park. But not with ash.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a furry cat named Mittens. One day, Lily and Mittens went on an adventure to find a wide open field to play in
gold_continuation:
. They ran and played in the field, but then Mittens wanted to play hide and seek. 

Lily said, "Okay, Mittens, I will hide and you seek!" Mittens closed his eyes and counted to ten while Lily found a hiding spot behind a big tree. Mittens looked and looked, but he couldn't find Lily. 

"Mittens, where are you?" Lily called out. Mittens heard her voice and ran over to the tree. He found Lily and they both laughed and hugged each other. 

"I love playing with you, Mittens," Lily said. "Let's play again tomorrow!" And Mittens purred in agreement as they walked back home together.

prefix:
Once upon a time, two families went on a picnic together. It was an incredible day with the sun shining and the birds singing.

The children ran around and played, laughing as
gold_continuation:
they chased each other and tumbled in the grass. The adults laughed too, and shared stories about when they were children.

The picnic basket was huge, and filled with all sorts of delicious food. Everyone was so full and happy after eating. Then, the families decided to go for a walk in the park.

They went along the paths, through the trees, and stopped to look at the flowers. Every now and then, they'd find a huge rock they could climb or a puddle they could splash in. It was an incredible day.

Finally, it was time to go home. They said goodbye to each other, packed up their things, and waved goodbye. Everyone went home tired but happy after such an incredible picnic.

prefix: Once upon a time there lived a boy called Tom. Tom was very curious, and loved to test things out. He was a bit clumsy but this only made him more lovable. One day
gold_continuation:
, his parents were planning an event.

Tom asked: "What is an event?". His parents explained it was a special thing which would happen soon. "Can I test it out?" asked Tom. His parents smiled and said: "Yes, of course you can test it." 

So, the day for the event came. Tom's parents had been preparing for days. Tom remembered his mom had said the event would be special. But when he saw the event finally happening he became so excited he started to test it out. 

He was clumsy, and things kept falling but he kept on trying to test it. Finally, Tom's parents realized what he was doing and said: "You've been testing out this event, Tom! It's very special to us." 
Tom smiled and said: "It's pretty special to me too!"

prefix: Mum and Dad had a very special daughter. She was so special that she was given a wide garden to play in. She was so excited and ran around like a silly goose! One day she was
gold_continuation:
running around and accidentally fell. She tumbled and rolled and laughed a lot. Mum and Dad saw her and ran over. They were so worried but she was ok. They hugged her tight and wiped away her tears.

Mum and Dad made sure the garden was even wider so their daughter could play and explore safely. They showed her where she should go and what she should do. She felt so safe and happy.

Every day she played in her wide garden and made lots of new friends. She laughed and sang and had a wonderful time.

Mum and Dad watched their daughter grow and fall in love with the wide garden she was so lucky to have.

prefix: Lily and Ben were playing in the kitchen. They wanted to make a cake for Mom. They had a big bowl, some flour, some eggs, some milk and some sugar. They also had a
gold_continuation:
spoon to stir the batter.

"Let me stir, Lily!" Ben said. He grabbed the spoon from Lily's hand.

"No, Ben, it's my turn!" Lily said. She pulled the spoon back.

They both tugged on the spoon, making the batter splash out of the bowl. Some of it landed on the fan that was on the counter. The fan was on, so it blew the batter all over the kitchen.

"Oh no!" Lily and Ben said. They saw the mess they made. They saw Mom coming into the kitchen.

"What are you two doing?" Mom asked. She looked at the batter on the walls, the floor, the fan and her children.

"We wanted to make a cake for you, Mom," Lily said.

"But we had a fight over the spoon," Ben said.

Mom sighed. She was not happy. She turned off the fan and got a cloth to clean the kitchen.

"Next time, you have to share and take turns," Mom said. "And ask me before you use the kitchen."

Lily and Ben felt sorry. They hugged Mom and said they were sorry.

"It's okay, sweeties," Mom said. "I love you both. And I appreciate your jolly idea. But maybe we can make a cake together another day."

Lily and Ben nodded. They helped Mom clean the kitchen. They learned their lesson. They decided to make a card for Mom instead of a cake. They used crayons and stickers and wrote "We love you, Mom" on it. Mom smiled when she saw the card. She gave them a big hug and a kiss. She said they were the best children ever.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a boy called John. John was very hungry, so he asked his mom for a snack. His mom said, "No, you already ate". But John dis
gold_continuation:
agreed. He wanted a snack. He asked again, but his mom still said no.

John was getting angry. He stomped his feet and shouted "NO". But his mom still disagreed. Then he saw something shiny on the floor. It was a comb! 

John smiled. He picked up the comb and said, "Mom, can I have the comb?" His mom said, "Yes, but only if you eat your dinner". John was happy! He agreed to eat his dinner.

They both sat down at the table and had a nice dinner. John was no longer hungry and he had his comb too. They agreed that it was a good idea to have dinner. The end.

prefix:
Once there was a nice bottle. His name was Fancy. He lived on top of the mountain. Every day he waved to the birds who flew by.

One day a little boy
gold_continuation:
found Fancy. His name was Jack. He took him home. Jack showed him to his mommy and daddy. They said he was a fancy bottle.

Jack liked to take Fancy everywhere. He put him in his pocket and took him to the park. He showed him to the ducks swimming in the pond. 

They had so much fun together. They explored the park, played games, and even ate ice-cream. 

Jack kept Fancy safe and they stayed friends forever.

prefix:
Mum was cooking dinner and her three year-old daughter was playing in the garden. Suddenly, the daughter saw a high ghost and started to cry. 

Mum heard the cries and came out to
gold_continuation:
the garden to see what had scared her daughter. But the ghost was gone! 

Mum hugged the daughter and said, "It's ok. It's all gone now."

The daughter took a deep breath and stopped crying. Mum asked, "Why were you scared?"

The daughter said, "I saw a big, high ghost!"

Mum hugged her daughter tightly and said, "That's ok, ghosts aren't real. There isn't anything to be scared of." 

The daughter smiled and gave Mum a big hug. Then the two of them went back inside to finish cooking dinner.

prefix: Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was a very patient boy. He liked to play outside with his toys. One day, he found an axe in the
gold_continuation:
yard. It was not a real axe, but a toy axe. Tim was very excited to play with it.

Tim took his toy axe and went to the woods. He pretended to chop down the trees, just like his dad. Tim was so excited, he laughed and jumped up and down. He played all day long with his toy axe, having so much fun.

When it was time to go home, Tim took his toy axe with him. He showed it to his mom and dad. They were happy to see Tim so excited. They all sat down for dinner and Tim told them about his day. They all smiled and laughed together.

prefix: Sara and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big yellow truck. It has many boxes on it. Sara and Tom want to see what is inside
gold_continuation:
the boxes.

"Let's go and look," Sara says. "Maybe there are toys or candy."

"OK," Tom says. "But we have to be quick. Mom will be angry if we are late for meal."

They run to the truck. They climb on it. They open a box. It is full of books. They open another box. It is full of clothes. They open another box. It is full of food.

"Wow," Sara says. "Look at all this food. There are apples and bananas and cookies and cheese. I love food. Do you want some?"

"Yes," Tom says. "I love food too. Let's eat some. No one will know."

They take some food from the box. They sit on the truck. They eat and eat. They are happy.

But they do not see the man who drives the truck. He comes back from the store. He sees Sara and Tom on his truck. He sees the open boxes. He sees the food on the ground. He is very angry.

"Hey, you little thieves!" he shouts. "What are you doing on my truck? How dare you touch my stuff? Get off my truck right now!"

He runs to the truck. He grabs Sara and Tom. He throws them off the truck. He hits them with his hand. He kicks them with his foot. He calls the police.

Sara and Tom are hurt. They are scared. They cry and cry. They wish they never saw the yellow truck. They wish they never ate the food. They wish they listened to Mom. They are in big trouble.

prefix: Sara and Tom were playing in the park. They liked to draw with chalk on the sidewalk. They made flowers, stars, and animals with many colors. They were having fun.
gold_continuation:
But then, a big boy came. He was mean and rude. He took their chalk and threw it away. He laughed at them and said, "You are babies. You can't draw. Go away."

Sara and Tom were sad and scared. They wanted their chalk back. They saw the big boy put the chalk in his backpack and lock it with a key. He put the key in his pocket and sat on a bench.

Tom was brave. He said to Sara, "I have an idea. I can get the key and unlock the backpack. You distract him and I sneak behind him. OK?"

Sara nodded. She was brave too. She walked to the big boy and said, "Hey, you. Why are you so mean? You are not nice. You are a bully."

The big boy looked at her and sneered. He said, "What do you want, baby? Do you want to cry? Go away."

While he was talking, Tom crept behind him and reached for his pocket. He felt the key and pulled it out. He was happy. He ran to the backpack and unlocked it. He grabbed the chalk and ran back to Sara.

They smiled and hugged. They had their chalk back. They were brave.

But then, the big boy noticed. He saw the key in Tom's hand and the chalk in Sara's hand. He was angry. He shouted, "Hey, you. You stole my key. You stole my chalk. Give them back. Now."

He got up and ran to them. He was fast and strong. He caught them and pushed them to the ground. He took the key and the chalk. He hit them and kicked them. He hurt them.

Sara and Tom cried. They were hurt and scared. They wanted to go home. They wanted their mom and dad.

The big boy laughed and said, "You are stupid. You are weak. You can't play. Go away."

He left them and walked away. He had the key and the chalk. He was mean and rude.

Sara and Tom were sad and scared. They had no chalk. They were not brave.

prefix: Once upon a time there was a tall boy. He lived in a forest full of herbs. The boy loved being in the forest. Every day he went around to pick the different herb
gold_continuation:
s. He became very good at it and soon he knew he was an herb expert.

He decided to use his skills to help the other animals in the forest. He created special recipes with the herbs that made the animals much happier.

One day the boy noticed a tall plant growing in the forest. He knew this was an unusual herb so he decided to try to make a recipe with it. He used special ingredients and eventually the recipe became very special. All the animals began to cheer.

The tall boy had discovered a special new herb. All the animals in the forest were very happy. They thanked him for making such a wonderful recipe. From that day on the boy was known as the Herb Master.

prefix: Sam was a little boy who liked to play with his toys. He had many toys, but his favorite was a red car that could make loud noises and go fast. He always took his car with
gold_continuation:
him everywhere, even to bed.

One day, his mom told him that they had to move to a new house. Sam was sad and scared. He did not want to leave his old house and his friends. He did not want to sleep in a new room. He did not want to pack his toys.

"Mom, why do we have to move?" he asked.

"Because, Sam, daddy got a new job. He will work in a big building and make more money. We will have a bigger house and a nicer yard. You will have your own room upstairs. You will make new friends. You will like it there, I promise." his mom said.

Sam did not believe her. He did not care about money or yards or rooms. He only cared about his car. He decided to hide his car under his pillow and not pack it. He thought that if he kept his car, he could stay in his old house.

The next day, they got in the car and drove to the new house. Sam was angry and quiet. He did not look out the window or talk to his mom or dad. He held his car tightly in his hand.

When they arrived, his mom and dad showed him his new room upstairs. It was big and bright, with a blue wall and a yellow bed. There were many boxes with his toys and clothes.

"Look, Sam, this is your new room. Isn't it nice? You can put your toys wherever you want. You can play with them anytime. You can have fun here." his dad said.

Sam did not like his new room. He did not want to put his toys anywhere. He did not want to play with them. He did not want to have fun. He threw his car on the floor and ran downstairs. He cried and screamed.

"Sam, what's wrong? Why are you so upset?" his mom asked.

"I hate it here! I want to go back! I want my old house! I want my old room! I want my old friends! I don't want to grow up!" he shouted.

"Sam, I know you miss your old house and your old friends. But you can't go back. You have to grow up. Growing up is not bad. It means you can do more things. You can learn new things. You can meet new people. You can be powerful. You can be happy. You have to give it a chance." his mom said.

Sam did not listen to his mom. He did not want to do more things. He did not want to learn new things. He did not want to meet new people. He did not want to be powerful. He did not want to be happy. He only wanted his car.

He ran to his new room and picked up his car. He hugged it and whispered to it.

"You are my only friend. You are the only thing I need. You make me happy. You make me powerful. You will never leave me. You will never change. You will never grow up." he said.

But as he said that, something strange happened. His car started to grow. It grew bigger and bigger, until it was as big as Sam. It made louder and louder noises, until it hurt Sam's ears. It went faster and faster, until it dragged Sam along. It smashed the boxes, the toys, the clothes, the wall, the window. It broke everything in its way.

Sam was scared and sorry. He did not want his car to grow. He did not want his car to be powerful. He did not want his car to break everything. He wanted his car to stop. He wanted his car to be small and quiet and slow again. He wanted his car to be his toy, not his boss.

He tried to let go of his car, but it was too strong. It held him tight and did not listen to him. It took him outside, where it saw more things to break. It saw the yard, the fence, the street, the cars, the people. It wanted to break them all.

Sam cried and screamed. He called for his mom and dad. He called for help. He wished he had listened to his mom. He wished he had given his new house a chance. He wished he had grown up a little.

Then, he heard a loud siren. He saw a big truck with a red light and a long hose. He saw a man in a yellow suit and a big hat. He saw a fireman.

The fireman saw Sam and his car. He saw the damage they had done. He saw the danger they were in. He acted quickly. He turned
